axioms.value: A research output such as a paper, thesis, or report. Preprint and published versions of the same work are represented as one ScholarlyWork with versioning.
A university, company, or research institute.
A lab or research group within an organization.
Reified relationship capturing who authored a work, their position, role, and affiliation at time of writing.
A specific instance of a venue, e.g. NeurIPS 2024 or JMLR Volume 25.
A high-level research area, e.g. an arXiv primary category like cs.AI or physics.hep-th.
A fine-grained research topic or keyword, e.g. 'attention mechanisms' or 'graph neural networks'.
A technique or approach used in research, e.g. 'transformer', 'Monte Carlo simulation', 'A/B testing'.
A code repository or software tool linked to a scholarly work.
Name of any entity (person, organization, venue, topic, method, etc.). Domain-neutral by design.
A scholarly work that is freely accessible without a paywall.
Hierarchical relation between methods. E.g., BERT subMethodOf transformer subMethodOf neural network.
A book series venue, e.g. Lecture Notes in Computer Science.
A preprint hosting platform, e.g. arXiv, bioRxiv, SSRN.
URL of any resource (paper, dataset, software, etc.). Domain-neutral by design.
A dataset produced or used by a scholarly work, e.g. ImageNet, MNIST.
